title: Decaloba silvestris
intro: Decaloba silvestris, also known as Wild Madder or Cuzco root, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its distinctive red or yellow flowers and woody roots. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Decaloba silvestris include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Decaloba silvestris is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their potential therapeutic effects on various health conditions.
taxonomy: Decaloba silvestris is classified within the family Passifloraceae and order Malpighiales. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Decaloba silvestris is a member of the Passifloraceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as passionflower.
